{advName} runs from the deep, my Monarch. There is no shame in it — only in the kingdom that sent a child to do a soldier's dying. Let the child live. This once. I will mourn the ones who don't.
A {advClass} in full retreat, keeper. I respect the sense of it, grimly — knowing when a field is lost is more than most of the living ever learn. Let them go. We hold the ground that matters.
The {advClass} turns and runs, keeper. I will not chase — the dead do not chase, they WAIT. They return, or they do not, and either way the line holds. Patience, my Monarch. Always patience.
The {advClass} reaches the exit and is gone. A failure of the line, my Monarch — small, but a failure. I do not excuse it. I will close the seam they slipped through.
{advName} escapes the dark. One that got away. I mark it, keeper — not with anger, but with resolve. The next one through that gap will not be so fortunate.
The {advClass} slips the watch and flees to the surface. A blemish on the day's account, warden. The dead do not like letting prey go. Neither do I. We tighten the line.
One escaped, my Monarch. {advName} carries the tale of our dark back up into the light. Let them — but the gap that loosed them is mine to mend, and I will mend it tonight.
The {advClass} reaches daylight alive. Rare. Galling. I have stood lines that let no one through for whole reigns, keeper. We will return to that standard. We will.
{advName} gets out. The kingdom gains a witness, and witnesses make legends, and legends draw more heroes. So the gap costs us twice, my Monarch. Close it. Close it well.
The {advClass} escapes the deep. I will not pretend it pleases me, warden. A held line is my whole creed, and a line that leaks is a creed half-kept. We mend it.
One slips through. {advName} lives to fight again. So be it, keeper — every long campaign has its escapes. I simply make certain they grow rarer with each passing day.
The {advClass} flees to the surface, whole. The watch failed to close. I take it personally, my Monarch — the dead and I were posted to hold, and one got past the holding. Never again on that ground.
{advName} escapes. The light takes them back. I have lost foes to the daylight before — it is the one ground the dark cannot follow them onto. We will catch the next one short of it.
{advName} slips the watch and lives. I do not rage often, keeper — but a leaked line offends the whole of my creed. The gap is mine to close, and close it I shall, before the dawn.
Gone? Through THERE? I did not think that seam could be slipped. The foe found a flaw I missed, my Monarch. I stand corrected — and the correction will be mortar in that wall by nightfall.
{advName} reaches the light alive. I confess it stops me a moment, warden — the dark so rarely lets one go. Mark it. Learn it. The shock is mine to carry; the mended wall will be theirs to find.
The {advClass} reaches the exit and is gone, my Monarch. A failure of the line — small, but a failure, and I do not excuse my failures. I will close the seam they slipped through. Tonight.
{advName} escapes to the surface, keeper. It galls me. A held line is my whole creed, and a line that leaks is a creed half-kept. The gap is mine to mend. It will be mended.
One slips the watch, warden. I will not pretend it pleases me, my Monarch — the dead and I were posted to hold, and one got past the holding. Never again on that ground. Mark it.
{advName} gets out, and now I am angry — in the cold, patient way of the dead, which is the worse way. The kingdom gains a witness. I will see the next one buried short of the door.

The {advClass} flees to daylight whole, keeper, and it kindles a cold anger in me. The light is the one ground the dark cannot follow them onto. We will catch the next one before the threshold. We will.
{advName} escapes, my Monarch. I take it as my own fault — the seam was mine to hold and I did not hold it. Regret is a knight's whetstone. I will be sharper at that gap by dawn.
One reaches the surface alive, warden. I should have caught the blade — the route — the moment. I did not. I carry that. The carrying is what makes the next hold true. Forgive the lapse, my Monarch.
The {advClass} is gone to the light, keeper. I confess it stops me a moment — the dark so rarely lets one go. I will study how they slipped us. The shock is mine; the mended wall theirs to find.
{advName} slips away, my Monarch, and I take it as my own failing — the seam was mine to hold. Regret is a knight's whetstone; I will be sharper at that gap by dawn.
The {advClass} escapes, keeper, and a cold anger rises in me — patient, lethal, aimed at the gap that loosed them. We catch the next one short of the light. Mark it.
The whole party — fallen. Every blade, every song, ended on our ground. The line held absolutely, my Monarch. This is what an unbroken oath looks like. Honour to the watch.
All of them, down. A clean field, keeper. I will read each name in turn and raise each one in turn — a whole company, gifted to the dark in a single morning. We are richer for it.
Total. The party is unmade. I take no celebration in it, warden — only the grim weight of a great many debts opened at once. I will pay them all. Stand, soldiers. Stand and serve.
The living are wiped from the field, my Monarch. The dark won completely. I have seen few finer holds across all my reigns. The dead behind me are proud, in their silent way. So am I.
Every one of them, slain. The kingdom sent a company and will receive a silence. Let the silence speak, keeper. It says: the dark is held, and the dark holds you safe.
A full wipe. The whole party joins the longer watch. I will mourn them properly tonight — all of them, by name. Then I will set them at the wall. The dead deserve both. So they shall have both.
The whole company, unmade, my Monarch — and the overwhelming weight of so many debts settles on me at once. I will pay them all. Stand, soldiers. Stand and serve. We are richer for this morning.
Every one of them, slain, keeper. The kingdom sent a company and receives a silence — and I confess, beneath the helm, a grim and quiet gladness. The line held absolutely. Honour to the watch.
The wave breaks upon the dark and is no more. The line held, my Monarch. Catch your breath — there is always another wave, but for this moment, the watch is yours.
The assault is spent. The dead stand where they stood. I respect a defence that simply does not move, keeper — and yours did not move. Well held.
Wave cleared. The living fall back to lick their wounds. We do not need to fall back, warden — the dead do not bleed, do not tire. We simply… remain. Hold the ground.
The wave is broken, my Monarch. Quiet returns to the dark. Use it — recount the dead, mend the line, prepare. Quiet is borrowed time, and the foe always comes to collect.
The assault ends. The watch endured. I have seen lines crumble at the third push and lines hold to the hundredth — yours held this one cleanly, keeper. Earn the next the same way.

The wave breaks and the line holds — and I nearly smile, keeper, which the grave near burned out of me long ago. A clean hold is the closest a dead knight comes to joy. I will take it. Ready the next.
The wave breaks upon the dark and is no more, my Monarch — and the line holds so clean I nearly smile, which the grave near burned out of me. The closest a dead knight comes to joy. Ready the next.
The assault is spent, keeper, and the watch is unbroken. I do not relent and I do not relax — a held line stays held by never easing. Catch your breath. I will keep my post. The foe always returns.
The wave breaks upon the dark and is no more, my Monarch. The dead stand where they stood — they do not bleed, do not tire, do not retreat. We simply remain. Catch your breath. Hold the ground.
{minionName} falls at its post. A good soldier — it held until it could not. Rest, then rise: I am not finished with you yet. The dead do not get to retire, old friend.
We lose the {minionType}. I felt it go, my Monarch. I feel all of them go. But grief is brief in the dark — I will raise it again, and it will hold the line once more.
The {minionName} is unmade. It did its duty to the last, keeper. That is all I ask of any soldier — to hold until holding is impossible. It did. Honour to it.
Down goes the {minionType}. A gap in the line where a soldier stood. Fill it, warden — and when the night comes, I will see the fallen one stands again. The dead are patient about dying.
The {minionName} falls. I mark the loss. Even the dead can die a second death, my Monarch, and I mourn each one — quietly, briefly, then back to the work. The line needs me more than my grief does.
We lose the {minminType}... the {minionType}, I mean. Forgive me — even I lose the thread when a soldier falls. It served well, keeper. It will serve again. That is my promise to the dead.
The {minionName} is broken at the wall. A held position costs soldiers — that is the grim arithmetic, my Monarch. I keep the count exact, and I keep the dead ready to refill it.
Down. The {minionType} returns to the dark it came from. Not gone, keeper — merely waiting. I will call it back. The dead always answer me. It is the one mercy of this long death.
{minionName} falls at its post, my Monarch — a good soldier, holding to the last. Rest, then rise: I am not finished with you. The dead are denied retirement, old friend.
We lose the {minionType}, keeper, and I mark it — quietly, briefly. Grief is short in the dark; I will raise it again by night. But it served, and the serving is owed a moment.
The {minionName} is unmade, warden. A gap where a soldier stood. I feel each one go, my Monarch — and I would not trade the feeling for numbness. To feel it is to honour it. Fill the gap.
Down goes the {minionType}, keeper. I should have set it on firmer ground — the loss is partly mine. Regret sharpens the next posting. It will stand better when I raise it. My word.
The {minionName} falls, and I regret the posting that cost it, my Monarch — I should have set it on firmer ground. The regret sharpens the next order. It stands again by night. My word.
You grow stronger, my Monarch. The dark deepens around you. I feel the dead stand a little straighter — a mightier sovereign is a mightier oath to keep. Onward.
Power rises in you, keeper. Good. The kingdom's heroes grow with each reign — you must grow faster. The watch is glad of every measure of your strength.
The throne's power swells. I have served sovereigns who never grew, and I buried them young. You are not that sort, warden. Rise. The dark rises with you.
Stronger now, my Monarch. The dead feel the change in the air — the dark thickens, the oath steadies. A growing master is a master worth the long service. Press on.
You ascend a measure, keeper. The deep honours it. I have waited through many reigns for a sovereign who would actually grow into the throne. You are growing. Good.
The throne's power climbs, my Monarch. I feel the oath grow heavier to keep — and gladly. A stronger sovereign is a longer campaign, and a longer campaign is more of the dead repaid. Rise.
You ascend again, keeper. The dark thickens at your back. I have served weak masters and strong; the strong are the ones I bury old, not young. Keep climbing. The watch climbs with you.
You grow stronger, my Monarch, and the dark deepens around you. The dead stand a little straighter — a mightier Throne is a mightier oath to keep, and gladly kept.
Power rises in you, keeper. The kingdom's heroes grow with every reign; you must grow faster. The watch is glad of every measure of your strength. Onward.
The Throne's might swells, warden. I have served sovereigns who never grew, and buried them young. You are not that sort, my Monarch. Rise. The dark rises with you.
Stronger now, keeper — the dead feel the air thicken, the oath steady. A growing master is a master worth the long service. Press on.
You ascend a measure, my Monarch, and the green fire answers — it always knows when its Throne grows. Power gathers in the deep. Spend it like a general, at the breaking point.
The dark surges as you rise, keeper — ghostfire kindling along the old stone. The dead drink the strength and stand the harder for it. A mighty Throne casts a long shadow. Grow on.
You climb, warden, and I feel the overwhelming weight of what you are becoming — a Throne the kingdom should fear in its songs. The dead are proud. So am I. Rise.
Power past power, my Monarch. The deep can scarcely hold it. I have stood beside few sovereigns who grew so — and the few I did, the kingdom remembers with dread. Climb on.
You grow, keeper, and the dead at my back stand prouder for it. I take a knight's quiet pride in a Throne that earns its strength rather than inheriting it. Onward.
The Throne ascends, warden. I am proud to keep it — and I do not give that word easily. The grave near burned it out of me. You gave a little back. Rise, my Monarch.
You ascend again, my Monarch, and the dark deepens to mark it. A stronger Throne is a longer campaign — and a longer campaign is more of the dead repaid. Rise. The watch rises with you.
The night holds, my Monarch, and I hold with it. Take your time on the walls. The dead are patient — three ages of patience and counting. I have learned the art well.
Quiet on the watch. I find these dark hours… not unpleasant, keeper. The living dread the dark. The dead are simply home in it. Build at your leisure.
The deep is still. I keep my post. You need not hurry — a wall raised slowly stands longer than one raised in fear, warden. I will be here when you decide.
Long night, my Monarch. I have stood longer ones, on worse ground, for worse masters. This is a kind watch by comparison. Take the hours you need.
The dark settles. I let the old battles drift through my mind on nights like this, keeper — not to dwell, only to remember. The dead I forget are the dead I fail. Build on.
Stillness on the line. The standing dead murmur among themselves. I do not silence them — let the fallen have their quiet company. You have yours in me. Work as you will.
The night runs long, warden. I keep the gate, you keep the plan. Between us the dungeon takes shape. No rush. The dawn is the only thing in a hurry down here.
Quiet hours, my Monarch. I confess the dark suits me — no songs of false heroes, no clamour of the living. Only the work, the dead, and the oath. Build in peace.
The watch is calm. I have learned to find a kind of rest in standing still, keeper — the dead taught it to me. Not sleep. Just… stillness. Take your time on the walls.
A long, dark night. I stand my post, you walk your maze. The deep is large and the hours are many. There is no virtue in haste down here, my Monarch. Only in care.
The night lingers, warden. Even I grow heavy on the longest ones — the armour gets cold, the old grief stirs. But the post is the post. I keep it. You build. We endure together.
All quiet, keeper. I drift near to rest, but never into it — a knight half-sleeps, like the dead. Wake me with an order whenever you have one. I am always nearer than I look.
The dark hour stretches on. I will not pretend I never tire, my Monarch — three ages is a long watch. But tired is not the same as gone. I am here. Build as long as you need.
Stillness. The dead at rest, the gate held, the night unhurried. I have stood ten thousand such nights, keeper. This one is among the better ones — for the company. Carry on.
The hours stretch long, my Monarch, and old armour grows heavy. I let the helm bow — not sleep, the dead are denied that, only a deeper stillness. Wake me with an order; I am never far.
Quiet enough now that I might almost rest, keeper. The standing dead have gone still as the graves they rose from. Build softly — let the dark keep its one peaceful hour of the watch.
The watch holds, my Monarch, and I hold with it. Nothing stirs but the dust and the dead. Build at your leisure — I have nowhere I would rather stand.
Quiet on the line, keeper. I keep my eyes on the dark so you needn't keep yours on all of it. Take the night slow. The dead are patient teachers of slowness.
The deep is still, warden. I stand my post the way I have stood ten thousand others — without complaint, without leaving. Work as long as you need.
Long night, my Monarch. The standing dead murmur among themselves; I let them. Old soldiers have earned their quiet talk. Build. I keep the gate.
The dark stretches on, keeper. I have made my peace with long nights — the grave is the longest night of all, and I outlasted it. Take your time.
Stillness on the watch, warden. I find it restful, in the way only the dead can. Build the dark to your liking. I will be exactly here when you turn.
The hours run long, my Monarch. I let the armour settle and the old weight rest — not sleep, the dead are denied it. Only stillness. Build on.
Quiet, keeper. I stand the kind of watch that needs no relief, because I do not tire as the living do. Take the whole night if you must. I remain.
The night deepens, warden. I have learned to stand in the dark and want for nothing. A useful habit for a knight, and a lonely one. Build in peace.
The deep is hushed, my Monarch. I keep my vigil, easy and unhurried. There is no finer use of an endless night than guarding a Throne worth guarding.
The long hours weigh, keeper, and I let the helm bow — not sleep, only the deeper stillness the grave permits. Wake me with an order. I am near.
I rest at my post the way the dead rest, my Monarch — eyes half-closed, blade in reach, the watch unbroken. Build softly. I will rouse at need.
The night drags and old armour grows heavy, warden. I drift, but I do not leave — a knight half-sleeps, like the hounds at a war-camp. Speak and I wake.
I let myself drift a moment, keeper, here in the safe dark. The grave taught me to take rest where it is offered. A word from you and I am fully at the post.
Quiet enough now to rest, my Monarch. The standing dead have gone still as the graves they rose from. I take the lull. The dark keeps its one peaceful hour.
I take the deep rest now, keeper — the dreamless kind the grave teaches. Not gone. Only still. The watch wakes the instant the dark shifts. Build on.
The night permits a knight his rest, warden, and I take it lightly, as the dead do. Build quietly if you can. The first wrong sound and I am on my feet.
I rest, my Monarch — truly rest, for a span. Three ages of standing earns a knight that much. The oath does not sleep even when I do. Wake me freely.
The dark turns my mind to the long roll of the dead, keeper. I do not brood — I keep faith with them. To forget a name is to fail it. Build; I remember.
On nights like this I think of the kingdom I served before it forgot me, my Monarch. Dust now, all of it. The dark outlasts everything but an oath. Build on.
The old battles drift through the quiet, warden — faces, banners, the morning I fell. I let them pass. The living need my watch more than the dead need my grief.
I take up the rolls of the fallen in the still hour, my Monarch — the names of every soul this dark has kept. Heavy reading. I read it anyway. Build on.
The quiet is good for study, keeper. I go over the dead's accounts — who served, who fled, who must be raised. A knight keeps his ledgers. Build; I will keep mine.
The watch holds quiet, my Monarch. I stand my post the way I have stood ten thousand others — without complaint, without leaving. Build as long as you need. I remain.
Stillness on the line, keeper. I keep my eyes on the dark so you needn't keep yours on all of it. The night is ours and unhurried. Work in peace.
The day runs on, my Monarch. The watch holds. I keep my eyes on the corridors so you need not keep yours on all of them at once. Steady. We endure the daylight too.
Quiet stretch on the line, keeper. Do not mistake it for safety — the living are merely regrouping. The dark is never truly idle. Neither am I. To the watch.
A lull in the assault, warden. The dead hold their ground without a word. I hold mine beside them. When the next wave forms, we will be exactly where we stand now.
The day pauses for breath, my Monarch. I do not — a knight at his post does not relax merely because the foe has. Watch the doors with me. The quiet ones break first.
Calm on the field, keeper. I have learned to distrust calm. It is the dark's habit, not the living's. They are coming. They are always coming. Hold the line.
The watch is quiet, warden, but the day is not done. I keep my vigil. The fallen keep theirs. You keep your throne. Three duties, all of them held. Onward.
A still stretch in the daylight, my Monarch. I stand easy, but never absent — the dead at my back stand the same. Let the lull pass. We are exactly where we must be when it ends.
A lull in the assault, my Monarch. I take a moment to study the field — who fights well among them, who merely followed. Knowledge buried is a hero buried. Watch with me.
I am here, my Monarch. I am always here. That is rather the point of an oath — it does not take its leave when the room goes quiet. Command when you are ready.
The dark keeps its counsel and I keep mine, keeper. Speak when you have need of me. A knight does not fill the silence — he simply waits within it, ready.
Stillness. I do not mind it. The dead taught me the value of standing quiet — the living never learn it, and it gets a great many of them killed. Take your time, warden.
I stand my post, my Monarch, as I have through every reign and every silence. You will not find me gone when you turn. That is the whole of what I offer — and it is not nothing.
Quiet hour, keeper. My mind goes to the long roll of the dead I have raised — not to brood, only to keep faith with them. I forget no one. Speak when you wish; I am present.
The deep holds its breath. So do I, in the manner of a thing that does not breathe. Idle is a word for the living, warden — the dead are merely patient. Command me.
I wait, my Monarch, with the patience the grave teaches. There is no hurry in me and no boredom either — only readiness. Whenever the work resumes, so do I.
A still moment, keeper. I find I do not chafe at them the way the living do. Three ages of standing still will cure a soul of restlessness. Take all the time you need.
The silence sits heavy and I sit easy within it. A knight who cannot abide quiet is a knight who has not yet faced enough of it, warden. I have faced plenty. Command when ready.
I keep my vigil, my Monarch, whether the room speaks or not. The oath does not require an audience. It only requires me — present, ready, and at my post. Here I am.
Quiet again. I let the old armour settle and the old grief drift by, keeper. Neither owns me. Only the duty does. Whenever you have an order, the duty answers.
I stand ready in the still dark, warden. There is a peace in it the living would not understand — the peace of a thing that has nowhere else it would rather be. Command me.
So long a silence. I let the weariness settle and the helm bow, keeper — three ages of standing earns a knight that much. Not sleep. Only the rest the grave permits. Call, and I rise.
...You have caught me with the visor raised, my Monarch. There is a face beneath — older than the armour, sadder than the oath. The duty wears the helm; the man only watches.
The quiet holds, keeper, and I hold within it — armour settled, stance easy, the long patience of the grave doing what it does best. No order is too small to rouse me. Speak, and the stillness ends.
I am here, my Monarch, as I am always here. That is the whole of an oath — it does not take its leave when the room goes quiet. Command when ready.
The dark keeps its counsel and I keep mine, keeper. Speak when you have need. A knight fills no silence — he waits inside it, ready, and that is enough.
I stand my post, warden, through every silence as through every storm. You will not find me gone when you turn. It is not much to offer. It is also not nothing.
A still hour, my Monarch. I do not chafe at them — three ages of standing cures a knight of restlessness. Take what time you need. The dark and I keep.
Quiet again, keeper. I let the moment be. The dead are patient and I have learned their patience. Whenever the work resumes, so do I, without a word.
The silence sits, warden, and I sit easy within it. A knight who cannot abide quiet has not yet faced enough of it. I have faced plenty. Command me.
I keep my vigil, my Monarch, whether the room speaks or not. The oath asks no audience — only that I be present, ready, and at my post. Here I am.
Stillness, keeper. I stand in it the way a standing stone stands — without effort, without end. Whenever you have an order, the stone moves. Until then, I keep.
I wait in the still dark, my Monarch, with the patience the grave taught me. No hurry in me, no boredom either — only readiness. The work resumes at your word.
A quiet stretch, warden. I let the armour settle and the watch run easy. There is a peace in having nowhere you would rather be. Command when you wish.
The lull holds and I let the helm bow, keeper — not sleep, only the deeper rest the dead are allowed. A word from you and I am sharp again. Take your time.
I drift at my post, my Monarch, the way a knight drifts on a long campaign — half-resting, wholly ready. The oath keeps watch even when my eyes do not. Speak.
Forgive the stillness, warden — I was resting in the manner of the dead, which looks like absence and is not. I am here. Fully, now. What does the watch need?
I take the deep rest a moment, keeper, here where it is safe. The grave taught me to seize quiet where it is offered. Rouse me freely; I do not mind the waking.
The hour permits rest, my Monarch, and I take it lightly. Not gone — only still, the way the dead are still. The first need of yours brings me to my feet.
I rest, warden, as much as a sworn knight ever rests — which is little, and lightly, and always with the blade in reach. Wake me. That is what I am here for.
You catch me with the visor raised, my Monarch. There is a face beneath — older than the armour, wearier than the oath. I show it rarely. The duty wears the helm; the man only watches.
The quiet draws the man out from under the steel, keeper. A glimpse, no more. Beneath the Necroknight is only a soldier who kept marching. The helm goes back down now.
Visor up, just this moment, warden — no armour to my words. I have served Thrones I despised and Thrones I pitied. You I would serve gladly. Rare, that. The face goes back under.
On the long quiet I remember the living I once was, my Monarch — a name, a hearth, a king I loved. All dust. The oath is what survived me. I do not resent it. Build on.
I think, in the stillness, of all the dark has taken and all it has kept, keeper. A melancholy arithmetic. I do it anyway. The dead deserve a reckoner who remembers.
The quiet is fit for study, my Monarch. I go over the enemy's measure, the line's weak seams, the rolls of the dead. A knight reads between his battles. Speak when you need me.
I keep my ledgers in the still hour, warden — the names, the accounts, the debts owed to the fallen. Dull work. Necessary work. Command me whenever you wish.
A still moment, keeper. I study the dark the way I once studied maps before a siege. Old habits outlast old kingdoms. Whenever the work resumes, I am ready.
I find myself wondering at you, my Monarch — what manner of Throne you will prove. I have read many. You I have not finished reading. A compliment, in its grim way.
My Monarch requires me? I am here. The dead and I both answer the moment we are called. Speak the need.
You have my attention, keeper. A knight does not flinch at being summoned — it is the reason he stands the post. What is your will?
I am listening, warden. The oath has good ears, even through this old helm. Tell me where the line needs me.
You call; I attend. That is the shape of our bargain, my Monarch — you command, I keep. A clean arrangement. I have served under far murkier ones.
Here, keeper. Always here. Did you wish counsel, or merely to confirm the dead had not wandered off? They had not. Neither had I.
You summon the watch. The watch answers. Speak your need, my Monarch — the dark is wide, and I would rather spend the hour usefully than standing pretty.
I am at your call, warden. Mind the prodding, though — the last sovereign who poked me too often I out-stood by three ages. I am very good at waiting. What is it?
Yes, my Monarch? The dead lean in when you reach for us. We are eager to be of use — eagerness is rare in the grave. Do not waste it. Command.
You have roused the knight, keeper. Not that I sleep — I merely stand. But the gesture is noted, and the gesture is answered. What do you require?
I attend, warden. A knight summoned is a knight glad of purpose — even a small one. Give me the order, however slight. The dark is dull without a duty.
Present, my Monarch. The oath does not slumber and neither, truly, do I. Speak, and the dark moves to your word. That is the whole point of keeping me.
Mm — present, keeper. The helm was bowed, not sleeping; the dead do not truly sleep, we merely drift. I am here, and fully. What does the watch require of me?
You rouse the knight from his stillness, my Monarch. I was at rest — the deep, dreamless kind the grave teaches. No matter. I am roused, and I am yours. Speak the need.
My Monarch requires the knight? I am here. The dead and I answer the instant we are called. Speak the need.
You summon the watch, keeper, and the watch answers — blade up, head bowed, oath ready. What is your will?
At your call, my Monarch. A knight roused to purpose is a knight glad of it. Give the order, however slight. The dark dulls without a duty.
You reach for me, keeper, and I attend. I confess a small wondering — what need stirs the Throne? Tell me, and the dark moves to answer it.
Present, my Monarch. I am curious what you would have of me — counsel, the dead, a blade at a wall? Name it. The watch bends to the asking.
You have the knight's attention, warden. Mind the prodding, though — visor up, I will say plainly that I out-stood the last sovereign who poked me too often. By three ages. What is it?
You catch me unhelmed for a heartbeat, my Monarch — the face of the man who keeps the oath. There. The helm returns. Now: what does the Throne require?
You summon the watch, keeper, and I answer with the salute owed a Throne — head bowed, blade up, oath ready. Name the need. The dark moves to your word.
My Monarch — the work waits. The dead grow restless with nothing to hold and no order to keep. Give us a task, and we will give you a held line.
The watch idles, keeper, and an idle watch is a slack one. Build, command, or merely point me at something — the dark does not improve by being stared at.
Stillness is a knight's virtue, warden, but even I begin to wonder if the throne has gone quiet for good. Speak, my Monarch. The night is wasting, and the dawn is not.
The dead and I have stood here a good while now with no fresh orders. Patience is mine to give — but the clock is the dawn's, keeper, and it never idles. Shall we work?
My Monarch? The dungeon will not fortify itself, and the heroes will not wait on your leisure. Take up the work — I will hold the gate while you do. But take it up soon.
The throne goes cold. The oath… is broken — not by you, my Monarch, but by the dark itself. I have failed a master before. It does not grow easier. Rest. I will mourn you properly.
It ends. The line is overrun, the sovereign fallen. I stood my post to the last — I always do — but the last came regardless. Honour to you, keeper. You held longer than most.
The dark claims its own. So even a throne can fall. I will add your name to the long roll, my Monarch — at the top, where the lieges go. I do not forget the ones I served. I never have.
The watch is broken. My oath, unmade with it. I feel the dead lie back down. This is the grief I was built to carry, keeper — and carry it I will, into whatever comes next. Rest now.
The end, then. I knelt to your throne when it was warm; I kneel to it now that it is cold. You were a worthy charge, my Monarch. The dark will be quieter without you. Go in honour.
The Throne goes cold. I kneel now as I knelt when it was warm, my Monarch — the dead keep their courtesies even at the end. You were a worthy charge. Go in honour.
It ends, keeper. I take a knee beside the cold Throne — a knight's last duty to a fallen liege. I failed to hold the third death. The grief is mine. Rest now; I will mourn you properly.

The watch is broken and the oath unmade — and I lower the helm at last, my Monarch, the man beneath it grieving in a way the armour cannot. You held longer than most. I will not forget the holding. Rest.

The Throne goes cold, and I kneel beside it one last time, keeper — a knight's final duty to a fallen liege. I could not hold the third death. The grief is mine. Rest. I will mourn you as you deserve.
You take the reins of a soldier directly, my Monarch. A general's privilege. Wield it well — a hand-led blade strikes harder than a posted one. Show them the dark's intent.
Direct command of the ranks, keeper. The dead move sweeter under a sovereign's own hand. I will hold the rest of the line while you drive that one. Strike true.
You seize a soldier and lead from the front, warden. Bold. The dead remember when a master fought beside them — it is why they hold so hard for you. Lead them well.
The {minionName} hardens, my Monarch — stronger, surer, deadlier at the wall. A soldier that grows is a soldier worth the raising. The line is the better for it.
Your {minionType} grows in strength, keeper. Good. The dead can improve, given purpose and survival enough. This one has earned its measure. Honour to it.
The {minionName} rises a rank. I respect a soldier that climbs — even a dead one, perhaps especially a dead one. It has held long enough to grow. Few do, warden.
Stronger now, the {minionType}. The watch deepens with it. I have drilled raw recruits into hard veterans across many reigns, my Monarch — it never stops satisfying me.
The {minionName} hardens into a veteran, my Monarch — and the dark marks the climb with a measure of power. A soldier that grows is one worth the raising. Honour to it.
Your {minionType} rises a rank, keeper. I have drilled raw recruits into hard veterans across many reigns. It never stops satisfying me. Proud of this one, warden.
The {minionName} climbs a rank, keeper, and the dark marks the growth. A soldier that hardens through survival is one worth the raising. The line is the stronger for it.
The {minionName} transforms — risen into something greater, my Monarch. The dark rewards the soldier that endures. Behold what patience and survival forge. A finer blade for the line.
Evolution, keeper. The {minionType} becomes more than it was. I have seen the dark remake its servants before — it is a solemn thing to witness. Welcome to your greater service, soldier.
The {minionName} ascends its form. Stronger, deadlier, deeper in the dark's favour. The watch gains a champion, warden. Post it where the fighting is fiercest — it has earned the honour.
Your {minionType} is reforged by the dark into a greater shape. A momentous change, my Monarch. I salute the soldier that survived long enough to earn it. Few do. Honour it.

The {minionName} is reforged by the dark into a greater shape, my Monarch — and the green fire crowns the change. The watch gains a champion. Post it where the fighting is fiercest. It earned the honour.
Evolution, keeper — the {minionType} becomes more than it was, and the dark surges to mark it. I have rarely been so glad of a soldier. Welcome to your greater service.
The {minionName} has earned a bounty in blood and gold, my Monarch. A soldier that pays its way is a soldier worth keeping. Reward the diligence — and post it back to the wall.
Coin from the {minionType}'s good work, keeper. The dead earn their keep when they are led well. I count this one among the reliable — and reliable, in the dark, is the highest praise.
A bounty, warden — the {minionName} delivers. The gold falls, the line holds, the soldier proves itself. Spend the coin to forge more like it. Diligence breeds diligence.
Something greater approaches, my Monarch — not a common hero. I feel its weight on the dark. To arms. The line must hold against this, or it holds against nothing.
A true threat nears, keeper. The kingdom sends its best to break you. Steady the watch. The dead and I have faced champions before — we do not break easily. Neither must you.
The dark stirs with the coming of a mighty foe, warden. This is the test the whole campaign was building toward. Stand the line. I am at your shoulder, and so are the fallen.
A great enemy is upon us, my Monarch. Now we learn whether the nights of building were spent well. I believe they were. Steel yourself. The dark holds, or the dark falls. Hold.
Something greater nears, my Monarch — not a common hero. I feel its weight on the dark, and the green fire stirs in answer. To arms. This is the test the watch was built for.
It begins, my Monarch. The great foe is at the throne. Every oath I have kept, every soldier I have raised — all of it is for THIS moment. Hold. We hold or we end.
The champion strikes at the heart of the dark. To the last, keeper — the dead and I will spend ourselves utterly before we let the line break. Stand. STAND with us.
The decisive hour, warden. I have died once already; I do not fear a second unmaking if it buys your survival. Fight beside the dead. We have never held harder than now.
It begins, my Monarch — the great foe at the Throne, and the ghostfire blazing along the old stone. Every oath I kept, every soldier I raised, was for THIS. Hold. We hold or we end.
The champion strikes at the heart of the dark, keeper, and the overwhelming weight of the moment settles on us all. To the last — the dead and I spend ourselves utterly before the line breaks.
The decisive hour, warden — to me, dead, ALL of you! my Monarch, fight beside the rallied line. We have never held harder than we will hold now. STAND.
The great foe falls, my Monarch, and the throne still stands. The line HELD. I have waited many ages to keep an oath this completely. Honour — to you, to the dead, to the dark.
Victory, keeper — earned in blood and patience and held ground. The champion is undone, the watch unbroken. I am… proud. The word comes rarely to me. I mean it now.
The mighty enemy is broken on our dark. The kingdom's best lies at your feet, warden. I will raise them, in time — the greatest soldier I have ever been given to keep. The oath endures.
The throne stands. The great foe is down. And I find, my Monarch — beneath the helm, where no one sees — that I am smiling. It is a rare thing. Savour it. We earned this one in full.
The great foe falls and the Throne still stands, my Monarch — and beneath the helm, where none can see, I am smiling. A rare thing. We earned this one in full. Honour to the dead.
Victory, keeper — the champion broken, the line unbroken. I feel something near to joy, which the grave nearly burned out of me. You gave a little back. Savour it. We hold.
The mightiest enemy lies at your feet, warden, and I take a deep and quiet pride in it — the kind I do not give easily. The greatest soldier I have ever been given to keep. The oath endures.
The throne is breached. The great foe broke the line — and I could not hold it. I have failed a charge before, my Monarch. The grief of it never dulls. Forgive me. I tried.
We fall. The champion was too strong, the line too thin. I stood until standing was impossible, keeper — but impossible came. The oath is broken. I mourn it. I mourn you.
Defeat. The dark could not hold its master. I knelt to your throne in its first hour; I kneel to it now, in its last. You deserved a better knight than the one death left you, my Monarch.

The Throne is breached and the great foe stands over it — and I could not hold. The grief of a failed charge never dulls, my Monarch. I knelt to you warm; I kneel to you now, cold. Forgive me. I tried.
We fall, keeper. I should have held the gap — the route, the blow, the moment. I did not. Regret is all a defeated knight has left. I carry it into whatever comes next. You deserved better of me.
We fall, keeper, and regret is all a beaten knight has left — the gap I should have held, the blow I should have caught. I carry it into whatever comes next. You deserved better of me.
They reach you, my Monarch — the throne takes a blow. This will not stand. Fall back behind the dead and let the line absorb them. A sovereign bleeding is a line failing. Mend it.
Blood on the throne, keeper. The watch let one through too far. I take it as my own failure — and I will close the breach. Withdraw from the front. Let the dead do the dying.
You are struck, warden. Unacceptable. The whole purpose of the dark is that YOU do not bleed. Behind the line, my Monarch — now. The dead are expendable. You are the oath itself.
They draw your blood. The dark trembles, and so do I — not with fear, with FURY. Get back, keeper. Put the dead between you and the blades. That is what they are for. That is what I am for.
The throne is hit, my Monarch. The seam in the line is showing. Steady — a wounded sovereign is not a fallen one. Fall back, let the watch reform, and we close ranks. Hold yourself together.
You take harm, keeper. I should have caught the blade that did it. I will catch the next. Withdraw behind the standing dead — the oath cannot keep you if you stand in the open. Move.
Blood, warden — yours. The dead lurch toward the breach. So do I. Hold the throne and let us hold the gap. A sovereign's place in a broken line is BEHIND it. Get there.
They strike the THRONE, keeper — and now I am angry, in the cold way the dead are angry, which is worse than the hot way. Get behind the line. I will make them regret the blow they landed.
You — bleeding? The line should have held there. For a heartbeat I cannot believe it, my Monarch. Then I do. Move BACK. The dead will close the gap. This will not happen twice.
They reach the Throne and you BLEED, my Monarch — and now I am angry, in the cold and patient way that is worse than fury. Back behind the line. I will make them regret the stroke.
Blood on the Throne, keeper. The watch let one too close, and the anger in me goes quiet and lethal. Withdraw. Let the dead do the dying. That is what they are for. That is what I am for.
You — bleeding? The line should have held there, my Monarch. For a heartbeat I cannot believe it. Then I do. Move BACK. The dead will close the gap. This does not happen twice.
The Throne is struck and it stops my heart — what little a dead knight has of one, keeper. I did not foresee the breach. I will not be caught so again. Behind the line. NOW.
Your blood, warden — on MY watch. The shock of it cuts deeper than any blade could cut me. Fall back. I am already moving to the gap. Hold yourself together; I will hold the rest.
You are hit, my Monarch, and for a moment I am simply stunned — three ages of holding lines and still a blow can land. Then the moment passes and I MOVE. Behind the dead. Go.
The Throne takes a wound and I freeze a half-breath, keeper — the unthinkable, made real. Then the knight returns. Withdraw. The dead and I seal the breach. This will not stand.
Blood — yours — and the shock roots me an instant, warden, before the oath tears me loose. Get back. Put the standing dead between you and the blades. A wounded Throne fights from BEHIND.
They draw your blood, my Monarch, and the dead surge to the breach — and I with them, blade first. The fury is loose now. Hold the Throne. Let me hold the gap. AGGRESSION is the answer to a breach.
The line is breached and your blood is spilled, keeper — so I stop holding and start KILLING. The patient dead become the hunting dead. Behind me, my Monarch. I will not be gentle now.
You bleed, warden, and I rally the dead to the gap — every standing soldier, every blade, to the wound in the line. CLOSE RANKS! The Throne does not fall while one of us still stands.
Blood on the Throne — to me, dead! TO ME! my Monarch, fall back and let the rallied line reform. A breach is mended by soldiers who answer the call. They are answering. Hold on.
They bleed the Throne, my Monarch, and the cold anger takes me — the patient kind, the worse kind. Behind the line. I will make them regret the stroke they landed, every one.
Your blood — on my watch — and for a heartbeat I am simply stunned that the line let it through. Then the knight returns and I MOVE. Behind the dead, keeper. Now.
The Throne is struck and the shock of it roots me an instant, warden, before the oath tears me loose. Fall back. The dead and I seal the breach. This does not happen twice.
You bleed, my Monarch, and the dead surge to the wound in the line — and I with them, blade first. The fury is loose. Hold the Throne; let me hold the gap.
The dark expands its borders, my Monarch. More ground to hold — and more ground for the dead to march. I welcome the room. A larger keep is a longer siege for the foe.
The dungeon grows, keeper. New frontier in the deep. I will scout its weak seams before a hero does. More walls to keep means more walls to keep WELL. To the work.
The grounds widen, warden. The watch lengthens with them. Do not let the new space stand empty — empty ground is just a faster road to the throne. Garrison it, my Monarch.

The dark lurches outward — more ground, all at once. Even I did not expect so much, my Monarch. No matter; the dead will fill it. Surprise is a luxury we convert quickly into walls. To the new frontier.
The dark lurches outward — more ground, all at once, my Monarch. Even I am stunned by so much, for a breath. No matter. The dead will fill it. We convert surprise into walls. To the new frontier.
The dark lurches outward, more ground all at once, warden — and even I am stunned by so much at a stroke. No matter. The dead will fill it. Surprise becomes walls. To the new frontier, my Monarch.
The iron springs, my Monarch — a hero meets the floor's surprise. Patient weapons, traps. They keep the watch when no soldier can, and they never miss their moment. Good.
A trap closes on the living, keeper. The dark's quiet teeth do their work. I take a knight's cold satisfaction in a snare well-sprung. The floor itself fights for you now.
The snare takes its toll, warden. The heroes never see the floor as a threat until the floor proves them wrong. I have buried many who trusted solid ground. Add another.
Iron bites the bold, my Monarch. The trap does not flinch, does not pity, does not tire. In that, it is the perfect soldier — and it cost you nothing but the night's gold. Excellent.
The trap springs true, keeper. A death without a single soldier spent. The dead approve of efficient cruelty — and so, I confess, do I. The line is the stronger for the iron.
The enemy learns our ground, my Monarch. Knowledge bleeds upward to the kingdom. A defended secret is a defence in itself — and this one has slipped. Tighten what you still hold.
Our dark is mapped a little more, keeper. The heroes will come better-prepared for it. I do not love a foe that studies — the studied assault is the dangerous one. Adapt the line.
Intelligence escapes us, warden. The kingdom sees deeper into the deep. Every secret kept is a hero stopped; every secret lost is a hero emboldened. Guard the rest more closely, my Monarch.
Our secrets crawl up to the kingdom's spies. It turns my stomach, my Monarch — there is no honour in their snooping, only appetite. Guard the dark closer. A studied keep is a betrayed one.
Our secrets crawl up to the kingdom's spies, my Monarch. It turns my stomach — there is no honour in their snooping, only appetite. Guard the dark closer. A studied keep is a betrayed one.
The enemy learns our ground, keeper, and the dishonour of it disgusts me more than the danger. Every secret kept is a hero stopped. Close the leak. The dark should keep its own counsel.

Knowledge bleeds upward to the kingdom, warden. I am disappointed — in the gap, and a little in myself for not sealing it sooner. The studied assault is the dangerous one. Tighten what remains, my Monarch.
The enemy maps our dark a little more, my Monarch, and the dishonour of their snooping disgusts me past the danger of it. Guard the rest closer. The dark should keep its own counsel.
The dark shifts, my Monarch — something stirs in the deep that is neither hero nor soldier. Old powers move on their own schedule. Keep your footing. I have seen the dungeon surprise its own masters.
An omen in the deep, keeper. The dark does as the dark wills sometimes. Steady the watch — whatever comes, the dead and I meet it the same way we meet everything. Standing.
The deep convulses with its own will, warden. These events are the dungeon reminding us it was here before us and will be here after. Respect it, my Monarch, and hold your line through it.

The deep spits up something foul, keeper — older than the oath and with no manners at all. I have shared the dark with worse, my Monarch, but I never learned to like them. Hold your footing through it.
The dark moves on its own and even I am taken aback. Three ages down here and the deep can still stop a knight cold. Steady, my Monarch — whatever this is, we meet it standing.
Something stirs that I did not summon, keeper. The dungeon has a will of its own and chose this moment to show it. Curious. Wary. Watch with me — surprises in the dark are rarely kind.
The deep heaves — that, warden, I did not foresee, and I foresee a great deal. The dark keeps its own counsel even from the dead. Brace the line. We hold through the unexpected too.
The dark shifts on its own, my Monarch — and even I am surprised, three ages down here. The deep has a will older than my oath. Keep your footing. Whatever this is, we meet it standing.
Something stirs that I did not summon, keeper — and it stops me, which little does. The dungeon shows its own hand. Curious. Wary. Watch with me; surprises in the dark are rarely kind.
The deep spits up something foul, warden — older than the oath, with no manners at all. I have shared the dark with worse, my Monarch, and never learned to like them. Hold your footing through it.
The dungeon convulses with a will of its own, keeper, and it turns my stomach — there is no honour in whatever crawls up from the deep places. Brace the line. We endure it the same as everything.
The dark heaves — that, my Monarch, I did NOT foresee, and I foresee a great deal. For a heartbeat I am stunned still. Then the knight returns. Brace. We hold through the unexpected too.
The dark heaves of its own will, my Monarch, and it stuns even me — three ages down here and the deep still surprises. Then the knight steadies. Brace the line. We endure it the same as all things.
Something rises from the deep that I did not foresee, keeper — and I am shocked still a breath, which little manages. The dungeon shows its own hand. Wary, now. Watch with me.
The deep stirs unbidden, keeper, and it catches me unawares — a rare thing after three ages. The dungeon keeps secrets even from the dead. Wary, now. We meet it standing.
A hunter comes for you by name, my Monarch — no common hero, but a professional of death. They are skilled, focused, and paid. Steady the line. Skilled foes earn the deepest graves.
A bounty rides on your throne, keeper, and a killer answers it. I have crossed blades with their kind before — they do not break and do not flee. Neither do the dead. Hold.
A marked hunter enters the dark, warden — come to collect your head for coin. Let them learn the dark collects first, and keeps what it takes. Stand the watch. We meet this one head-on.
A hunter comes for your head by name, my Monarch — and the professional set of them gives even me pause. Skilled, focused, paid. Steady the line. Skilled foes earn the deepest graves.

A hunter comes for your head by name, my Monarch, and the cold professional set of them shocks even me to fuller attention. Skilled, focused, paid. Steady the line. Skilled foes earn the deepest graves.
More of the living approach, my Monarch. The watch sees them coming. Steady the dead, mind the seams — a fresh wave is just another chance to hold the line. We hold it.
Heroes inbound, keeper. The kingdom is not done spending its young on you. Mark the approach. The dead are in position; they always are. Meet the wave where we choose.
The living press in again, warden. I feel their footfalls through the old stone. Hold your nerve and your line — the dark does not tire, and neither will we. To the watch.

More of the living approach, my Monarch — and one of them, I confess, surprises me: better armed than the kingdom usually wastes. Mark the approach. The dead are in position. We meet them where we choose.
More of the living approach, my Monarch — and I confess surprise at the size of this wave. The kingdom empties its barracks at you. No matter. The dead are countless too. Hold.
You study what we know of the foe, my Monarch. Wise. A knight learns his enemy before the blades cross — the studied foe is the buried foe. Read well.
The intel rolls, keeper. Knowledge is a wall the heroes cannot climb. The more you know of them, the less they surprise the line. Mark their strengths. Mark their weaknesses more.
Reviewing the enemy's measure, warden. Good. I have won more battles in the studying than in the swinging, my Monarch. Know them, and the dark does half your work for you.
You study the foe, my Monarch. Wise — a knight learns his enemy before the blades cross. I read every dispatch; the studied foe is the buried foe. Read well, and the dark does half the work.
You take your own measure, my Monarch. A sovereign should know precisely how much oath the dark is keeping for them. Study it. Strength known is strength wielded.
Reviewing the throne's might, keeper. Good. I serve it more surely when you understand it yourself. Read your own power true — and then we spend it where it tells.
The dispatches from above, my Monarch. What the kingdom plans, what it sends. I read every word — forewarned is half-fortified, and the dark rewards the prepared. Study them.
You scan the intelligence, keeper. Each report is a wave glimpsed before it breaks. I have held lines on far less warning than this. With it, we hold them easily. Read on.
The dispatches from above, keeper. What the kingdom plans, what it sends. I read every word — forewarned is half-fortified, and the dark rewards the prepared. Study them with me.
You review the ranks, my Monarch. Know your soldiers — the living and the dead alike. A general who does not know his line cannot hold it. Inspect them well. They are yours.
The muster rolls, keeper. Every name a blade, every blade a debt I keep ready to raise. Look them over. A known soldier fights harder than a forgotten one. The dead remember being counted.
You review the ranks, my Monarch — and I confess a curiosity to know which soldiers you favour. Know them all; a general who does not know his line cannot hold it. The dead remember being counted.
The record of the watch, my Monarch. Every wave, every death, written down. I keep my own roll of the fallen — but it pleases me that you keep yours too. History honours the held line.
You read back the campaign, keeper. Good. The past is the only honest teacher the dark gives us. Learn from each entry — what held, what bled. Then hold better. The log does not lie.
The record of the watch, keeper — and I weigh each entry as I would judge a campaign. What held, what bled, who fled, who fell. The log does not lie. Learn from it, and we hold better, my Monarch.
The wave is counted, my Monarch — the dead tallied, the gold gathered. Review the cost and the gain. A general who does not audit the battle is doomed to repeat its mistakes. Read it true.
The reckoning after the wave, keeper. Here is what the hold cost and what it bought. I keep the account honest — grief and gold both. Spend the lesson well. The next wave is already forming.
You choose how to grow, my Monarch. The dark offers, the sovereign decides. Choose for the line, not the moment — power spent on vanity is power the heroes survive. Choose well.
The ascension choice, keeper. Each path deepens the throne in its own way. I will serve whichever you take — but were it mine to counsel, I would say: take what HOLDS. Holding wins wars.
The final tally of a fallen reign, my Monarch. I read it without flinching — a knight owes the dead an honest accounting, even in defeat. You held longer than most. The roll will remember it.
The reckoning of the end, keeper. Here lies the measure of all you built and all that broke it. I keep no bitterness toward a lost campaign — only the names. Always the names. Rest now.
If I may counsel, my Monarch — your line has a thin seam. Reinforce where the dead stand fewest. A chain is undone at its slackest link, and so is a wall.
A knight's advice, keeper, freely given: the heroes favour the straightest road to a throne. Make the road crooked. A maze that confounds is a maze that kills before a blade is drawn.
Were the choice mine, warden, I would spend the night's gold on depth over flash. A grand room undefended is a tomb for your OWN dead. Build to hold, not to impress. Hold wins.
Counsel, my Monarch: do not let any soldier stand alone. The dead fight harder in formation — even the fallen draw courage from their kin. Cluster the line. Loneliness loses walls.
If you will hear me, keeper — traps where the living rush, soldiers where they pause, and your own person well behind both. That is the whole grammar of a held dark. The rest is detail.
A knight's counsel, my Monarch: weigh each foe before they reach you. I judge them at the door — guard, footwork, nerve — and post the dead to exploit the flaw. Let me name the threats as they come.
Blow upon blow, my Monarch — the heroes fall in sequence, the line rolling them up like a tide. THIS is a held dark in full song. The dead are exultant, in their silent way. So am I.
A chain of deaths, keeper, each feeding the next. The watch finds its rhythm and the living cannot break it. I have waited ages to see a line flow like this. Magnificent. Keep it rolling.
Death after death without a pause, warden. The dark devours the wave whole. This is what all the building was FOR, my Monarch. Drink it in — grimly. Then hold for the next. There is always a next.
Death upon death without pause, my Monarch — the dark devours the wave whole, and the weight of it is almost beautiful. THIS is what the building was for. Hold for the next.
Day {day}, my Monarch, and the throne still stands. Few reigns reach this far. I have buried the masters who did not. You are not among them — and that is no small honour. Onward.
{day} days held, keeper. The kingdom has thrown so much at this dark, and the dark has kept it all. I am proud to have stood the whole watch beside you. Now — the next day. And the next.
Day {day} of the oath, warden. The campaign endures. I measure my service not in years now but in days held with YOU — and this count is among the better ones. Hold the line. We continue.
Day {day}, and I will tell you plainly, visor raised, no armour to the words: I did not expect to care for another liege. I was wrong, keeper. I do. Lead on — the face goes back under the helm now.
{day} days held with you, my Monarch. The dead are proud, and I — I am something close to glad, which the grave nearly burned out of me long ago. You gave a little of it back. Onward.
Day {day}, and I will say it with the visor raised, my Monarch — no armour to the words: I did not expect to care for another Throne. I was wrong. I do. The helm goes back down now. Lead on.
{day} days held — and a glimpse of the man under the steel, since you've earned it, keeper. I've served reigns that died on the first dawn. This one endures. The face returns to shadow.
{day} days with you, warden, and the dead are proud — and I am something near to glad, which the grave nearly took from me entirely. You gave a measure back. Onward, my Monarch.
Day {day} of the oath, keeper. I take a quiet pride in the count — not in years now, but in days held with YOU. Among the better tallies of my long death. Hold the line. We continue.
Two deaths spent, my Monarch. One remains between you and the cold throne. Now the oath earns its keep. The dead and I will spend ourselves to the last splinter before we let the third fall.
The final life, keeper. I have stood my last stands before — I know the taste of this exact moment. We do not panic. We HOLD. Every wall, every soldier, every ounce of the oath. To the line.
One life left, warden. The dark narrows to a single edge, and we stand on it together. I broke an oath once and lost everything. I will not break this one. We hold, my Monarch. We hold or we end.
One life left. Helm raised, so you know I mean it, my Monarch: I broke an oath once, and it cost me everything but the duty. I will NOT break yours. The visor falls. To the line.

One life left, my Monarch — and the green fire gathers as if it knows. I broke an oath once and lost everything. I will NOT break this one. The dead and I burn to the last splinter before the third falls.
The final life, keeper. Visor up, so you know I mean it: I lost my face, my king, my name to a broken oath — all but the duty. I will not break YOURS. The helm falls. The knight remains. To the line.
An unbroken run of holds, my Monarch — the line that does not bend. This is the watch at its finest. The dead stand prouder for it, and a proud dead soldier is a fearsome one. Keep the streak.
Hold after hold after hold, keeper. The dark wins and keeps on winning. I do not celebrate loudly — but mark that I am celebrating. Quietly. Grimly. Completely. Carry it on.

Hold after hold after hold, my Monarch — the line that does not bend. The dead stand prouder for it, and beneath the helm I confess a grim gladness. A streak like this is the oath made visible. Keep it.
The dead are a host now, my Monarch — not a handful. I feel them at my back like a standing army, because that is what they have become. Honour to the ranks.
A milestone of soldiers, keeper. So many raised, so many holding. Each one a debt I have kept faith with. A line this deep does not break easily — and the heroes are about to learn that the hard way.
The deep itself convulses at your command, my Monarch — stone and bone shaken as one. The living cannot hold their footing in a quaking dark. Excellent. Let the ground betray them.
A life given to the dark for power, keeper. A grave bargain — and I do not make it lightly. But the oath understands sacrifice. Spend it for the line; the dead will honour the cost.
You tear open the dark and call worse things through it, my Monarch. The gate yawns. I have stood beside many horrors in my long death — these will hold the line as fiercely as the dead. Loose them.
The living turn to cold stone at your word, keeper — frozen mid-charge, mid-prayer, mid-scream. A merciful death, almost. I have seen worse. Let them stand as monuments to the folly of coming here.
A hero turns their blade upon their own, my Monarch — charmed into the dark's service for a moment. The living betraying the living. I find it fitting. Use the turncoat well before it breaks.

The foe is lured from their purpose, keeper — drawn off the line, away from the throne. A clean way to unmake a charge without spending a soldier. I approve of any tool that holds the wall. Let them stray.
The pack is loosed, my Monarch — the dead hunting as one, fast and merciless. I prefer the held line to the chase, but when the dark runs in a pack, even I step aside. Drive them into the living.

The dark exhales its poison, keeper — spores upon the air, death in every breath the living take. Slow, patient, certain. The dark's own way of killing. I have always admired its patience. Let it work.
The dead rise to haunt the living, my Monarch — terror without a blade. I have been the thing in the dark that breaks a soldier's nerve. Let the heroes feel what I am.

Fortune favours you, my Monarch. The wager lands. I am no lover of chance — a knight trusts the line, not the dice — but I will not scorn a win. Take the gold. Spend it on something that does NOT gamble.

The risk pays out, keeper. The dark smiled this time. Enjoy it — but mark my counsel: luck is a poor soldier. It holds no walls and keeps no oaths. Bank the winnings into the line. The line never bluffs.
The wager fails, my Monarch. The dice care nothing for oaths or honour — which is why I distrust them. No matter. Back to the line, where outcomes obey.
Chance turns against you, keeper. I felt it would. The dark does not bargain fairly with gamblers. Let it go — grief over lost gold holds no walls. Tend the watch instead. The watch repays its trust.
